name: Tea, Coffee and Cocoa (Meal Timing)

book_claims.claim: Consuming during meals reduces absorption of iron, zinc and other minerals
book_claims.mechanism: Polyphenols (tannins) in tea, coffee and cocoa chelate iron and other minerals, reducing their bioavailability

supplementary_research.finding: Polyphenol-containing beverages inhibited iron absorption by 20-73% in human studies. Black tea reduced absorption by 79-94%. Even herbal teas and cocoa showed significant inhibition. Effect was dose-dependent on polyphenol content.

Study in Indian women found tea consumption with meals reduced non-heme iron absorption by 62%. Waiting 1 hour after a meal before drinking tea largely eliminated the inhibitory effect, supporting the book's recommendation to avoid these beverages during meals.

Comprehensive review confirmed that polyphenols from tea, coffee, and cocoa chelate non-heme iron in the gut lumen. Vitamin C can partially counteract this inhibition. Heme iron (from animal foods) is not significantly affected.

culinary_pairings.source: Vitamin C counteracts iron chelation -- add lemon to tea or consume vitamin C-rich foods at meals
Traditional sweetener for tea and coffee across all cultures
Classic spice addition to all three beverages
Fresh ginger in tea; ginger-chocolate combinations
Arabic coffee and chai tea spice

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Varies: black tea ~4.4%, green tea ~3%, coffee ~1-2%, cocoa ~6%
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Bind non-heme iron in the gut lumen forming insoluble complexes. Effect is on iron consumed at the same meal, not on iron stores generally. Does not affect heme iron from meat/fish.

warnings: Consume between meals (at least 1 hour after eating), not during meals containing iron or zinc
This is especially important for plant-based diets where all iron is non-heme and more vulnerable to chelation
Vitamin C (from lemon, fresh chillies, or bell peppers) partially counteracts the inhibitory effect

see_also.coffee: Deep dive on chlorogenic acids, caffeine, and cardiovascular benefits
